overview: Based on the published agenda, the D.C. Everest Area School District Board of Education was scheduled to conduct a regular meeting addressing staffing changes, budget matters, policy reviews, and several administrative updates. The board was expected to consider multiple policy approvals, school handbook revisions, curriculum evaluations, and facility planning discussions.

agenda.item: Second Reading of Policies (po0100 Definitions, po0167.3 Public Comment at Board Meetings, po3120 Employment of Professional Staff, po3120.05 Employment of Personnel in Community Education Programs, po3140 Non-Renewal Resignation and Termination, po4120.04 Employment of Substitutes, po5200.01 Full-Time Student)
Set the Date for Annual Meeting/Budget Hearing
School Handbook Changes (Elementary, Middle School, Junior High, Senior High)
Review of po2261.01 - Parent and Family Engagement in Title 1 Programs
Annual Eval of Reading Curriculum po2131.01
First Reading of Policies (po1213, po3120.01, po3213, po4213, po7530.02, po7530.03, po7544, po8120, po8462)
WASB Fall Region Five Meeting Information

discussions.body: Shannon Grabko was scheduled to present on behalf of the Wisconsin Association of School Boards Legislative Network, providing updates on legislative matters relevant to the school district.
Larry Schaefer from Cooperative Educational Service Agency #9 was scheduled to present, offering regional educational updates and support information.

Superintendent Dr. Casey Nye was scheduled to present multiple reports including District Update, Certified Staff Compensation Planning, Internal Review of Student Screen Time, Facilities Study Learning Focus, Responsive Classroom, Summer Projects Update, and information about the 2026 World Horseshoe Pitching Championship.
The board was expected to review and potentially approve handbook changes for Elementary (Riverside Sample), Middle School, Junior High, and Senior High schools for the 2026-27 school year.

The Reading Specialist was scheduled to present the annual reading curriculum evaluation report including Reading Instructional Goals and Kindergarten Assessment updates for July 2026, as required by policy po2131.01.
The board was expected to consider a request regarding a Senior High student trip to Japan.
The board was scheduled to approve recommended employment, resignations, and contract adjustments for certified staff based on documentation submitted on July 8 and July 14, 2026.
The board was expected to review the Treasurer's Report, Balance Sheet, Budget Transfers (25-00473 through 25-00515), and Budget Revisions (25-00053 through 25-00062) for July 2026.
A bus accident report from June 18, 2026 was scheduled for board review.

The board was scheduled to conduct second readings on seven policies including Definitions, Public Comment at Board Meetings, Employment of Professional Staff, Employment of Personnel in Community Education Programs, Non-Renewal/Resignation/Termination, Employment of Substitutes, and Full-Time Student definitions.

The board was expected to conduct first readings on nine policies including Student Supervision and Welfare, Job Descriptions, Personal Communication Devices, Student Technology Acceptable Use and Safety, Use of Social Media, Volunteers, and Reporting of Child Abuse and Neglect and Sexual Misconduct.

discussions.item: Review of Parent and Family Engagement Policy
discussions.body: The board was scheduled to review policy po2261.01 regarding Parent and Family Engagement in Title 1 Programs.
The board was expected to approve a list of independent hearing officers for the district.
The board was scheduled to consider academic standards for the 2026-27 school year.

publicComment: A public comment period was scheduled as part of the regular board meeting agenda.
actionItems: Board was expected to approve the minutes from the June 17, 2026 meeting
Board was expected to approve certified staff employment, resignations, and contract adjustments
Board was expected to approve Treasurer's Report and financial statements
Board was expected to approve budget transfers and budget revisions
Board was expected to approve second readings of seven policies
Board was expected to conduct first readings of nine policies
Board was expected to approve independent hearing officers
Board was expected to set the date for the annual meeting and budget hearing
Board was expected to approve or modify school handbooks for all school levels
Board was expected to approve the Senior High trip to Japan
Board was expected to review and potentially approve academic standards for 2026-27
title: Regular School Board Meeting - 2026-07-15
